llm_pattern: The absolute current magnitude |I| decreases exponentially as voltage V approaches zero from negative values, spanning many orders of magnitude, and then increases symmetrically for positive voltages. The inverse of the absolute voltage 1/|V| decreases monotonically as |V| increases. The natural logarithm of |I|/V^2 decreases approximately linearly with increasing 1/|V|. The current magnitude |I| spans approximately 88 decades across the voltage range from -6 V to 6 V.

llm_keywords.keyword: exponential decrease of |I| near zero voltage
linear decrease of ln(|I|/V^2) with increasing 1/|V|
llm_keywords.evidence: ln(|I|/V^2) decreases from about -14.22 at 1/|V|=0.1667 to about -230.38 at 1/|V|=10
Maximum |I| ~ 2.4e-5 A at V=-6; minimum |I| ~ 8.9e-103 A near V=-0.1
